codeReview.summary: The commit is appropriately scoped to the install-UX cleanup: it removes the redundant empty `vera-core` feature arrays from `crates/vera-cli/Cargo.toml:14` and `crates/vera-mcp/Cargo.toml:10`, updates `README.md:23-63` to a single `cargo build --release` path with no `--features local` references, and preserves the existing runtime local-mode hooks already exposed in `crates/vera-cli/src/main.rs:94`, `:151`, `:178` and `crates/vera-core/src/config.rs:105-108`. I did not find a functional defect in this feature's implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission-level `fulfills` mapping for `simplify-install-ux` is broader than the change set and handoff evidence. This feature cleanly owns the UX/docs assertion (`VAL-INST-010`), but the mapped runtime assertions (`VAL-INST-004` and `VAL-INST-005`) are not implemented or directly exercised in this commit, which makes validation ownership harder to reason about.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json:507-511` maps `simplify-install-ux` to `VAL-INST-004`, `VAL-INST-005`, `VAL-INST-006`, and `VAL-INST-010`; commit `687ebd7` only changes `README.md`, `crates/vera-cli/Cargo.toml`, and `crates/vera-mcp/Cargo.toml`; the handoff at `/home/lamim/.factory/missions/291889bc-b994-4be9-95b4-d1a630b822cf/handoffs/2026-03-22T07-14-48-770Z__simplify-install-ux__340c07f7-c189-46fb-9709-15589aced4f3.json` lists help/grep/build/test checks, but no API-mode or lazy-model-download verification.

summary: Pass: `687ebd7` delivers the requested install-UX simplification by removing the redundant CLI/MCP Cargo feature forwarding and rewriting the README to a single build path without `--features local`, while the existing `--local`/`VERA_LOCAL` hooks remain intact. I noted one shared-state observation that the feature-to-validation mapping over-attributes runtime assertions beyond what this commit actually changes.
